prerequisites: A Coda API token (the auth_token credential) issued for your account.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single secret field, auth_token. Coda's API uses Bearer token authentication: the token is sent in the Authorization header as 'Authorization: Bearer <your API token>'. Provide your Coda API token as auth_token.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a Coda API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Coda account and generate an API token for your account. This token is the credential the connector uses to call the Coda REST API.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the API token in the required auth_token field. The connector sends it on every request as an Authorization Bearer header to authenticate with the Coda API.

setupSteps.title: Be aware of rate limits
setupSteps.body: The Coda API enforces per-user rate limits and returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests when they are exceeded. Coda recommends that clients detect 429 responses and back off and retry. Rate limits are subject to change.
